question.text: As per SEBI regulations, mutual funds must dispatch redemption proceeds to investors within:

answer.text: 10 working days of the redemption request
3 working days of the redemption request
30 working days of the redemption request
15 working days of the redemption request

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI regulations require that redemption proceeds must be dispatched within 10 working days of the redemption request. Dividends must be dispatched within 15 days of declaration. If there are delays, the AMC must pay the unitholder interest at 15% p.a., and this expense must be borne by the AMC (not charged to the scheme).
**The Trap:** 3 working days is the typical actual processing time for most funds but not the regulatory maximum. 15 days is the timeline for dividend dispatch, not redemption. 30 days is too long — the regulation is 10 working days.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. Investor service timelines are commonly tested regulatory facts in all MF certification exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In practice, most AMCs process redemptions in 1-3 working days for equity/debt funds and overnight for liquid funds. The 10-working-day limit is the regulatory maximum. If delays occur, remind investors they are entitled to 15% p.a. interest as compensation.
